title: What is this coffin on the moon business
display_title: What is this coffin on the moon business
wikitext:
[]: What is this coffin on the moon business?             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Feb 14, 2008 12:50 pm I have been going through some material, and it seems that there are these references of coffins on the moon which I have not managed to see. Can someone please point it out?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 14, 2008 1:06 pm i think they're just containers, not "coffins"

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Feb 14, 2008 1:10 pm Is there like a screenshot so I can see them clearly?

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Thu Feb 14, 2008 1:39 pm You should just download rebuild 1.0 and see for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Feb 14, 2008 1:42 pm It is easier if someone points it out.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Thu Feb 14, 2008 2:04 pm Yea but its good to have a copy downloaded anyways. I think its in one of the trailers so go on youtube and have a look for the bit where kawrou shows his creepy mug.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Feb 14, 2008 2:32 pm The Imperialist wrote: Is there like a screenshot so I can see them clearly?

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Fri Feb 15, 2008 1:16 pm Is all that red stuff blood or what, looks pretty freaky to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Feb 15, 2008 7:19 pm The red reminds me of the streak of blood on the moon after 3I. Foreshadowing perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 15, 2008 10:50 pm V wrote: i think they're just containers, not "coffins"

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 16, 2008 2:53 am Reichu wrote: V wrote: i think they're just containers, not "coffins"

[]: Boxes on the Moon             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri Aug 29, 2008 6:26 pm Final scene of Rebuild, we see Kawaoru emerge from a box on the moon. Beside him, there are other boxes, some closed, some opened. In fact, there are four boxes open. Four Angels have attacked NERV at this point in the movie series. The 3rd (Assuming white chalk giant thing = Angel), 4th, 5th and 6th. So then, do the boxes correlate to the amount of Angels? Then, we see a Seele monument talking to Kawaoru on the moon. Can we infer from this then, that Seele might be having something to do with the release of Angels to the surface? I dont know... just something I noticed. A screenshotty of the scene http://i101.photobucket.com/albums/m57/Dartz_IRL/box_kawaoru.jpg

[]: Re: Boxes on the Moon             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Aug 30, 2008 4:22 pm Dartz wrote: Final scene of Rebuild, we see Kawaoru emerge from a box on the moon. Beside him, there are other boxes, some closed, some opened. In fact, there are four boxes open. Four Angels have attacked NERV at this point in the movie series http://i101.photobucket.com/albums/m57/Dartz_IRL/box_kawaoru.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Aug 30, 2008 5:30 pm I already suggested that (I think in the "interesting stuff" thread). I'd dig it up, but I don't think there's anything more to discuss until we get more information.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 6:10 pm Thread merge; it's "Kaworu"; and NAveryW wrote: I don't think there's anything more to discuss until we get more information.

[]:             Quote Post by illeffeqt » Mon Sep 01, 2008 10:02 pm look at it this way, at least now you have another thread to flex your elite admin muscle in, right? lock this thread QUICKLY. god forbid anyone enjoy a moment of conversation that might be (gasp!) aimless or (DOUBLEGASP!) previously discussed in the history of this forum.

[]: Re: Boxes on the Moon             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Sep 02, 2008 2:06 am EvaCub wrote: I'd say it is safe to assume that Kaworu is again an angel maybe the fifth and that the boxes correspond with the angels

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 06, 2008 6:38 am illeffeqt wrote: Words

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 14, 2008 1:06 pm i think they're just containers,
